CARSHUNTER 1447 Execution Spec — Working/Pressure Sink (Draft)
- This file absorbs implementation pressure; keep the canonical reference frozen.
- When updating checklists: never edit an existing checklist block; always append a new version with date; always state the reason for change; never introduce new authority, invariants, or guarantees via checklist. Checklists may only reference existing invariants; they may not introduce new STOP conditions or authority requirements.

Known gaps and defects
- `pms_govern/gcc_engine.py`: `GovernEngine.__init__` references `command` before assignment; ledger/tier/exposure logic still TODO. Fix without skipping schema/signature validation.
- CLI demo [pms_govern/cli.py](pms_govern/cli.py) is stubbed (`evaluate_exposure` missing, instantiates `GovernEngine()` without required args). Wire it to the real engine before use.

Operational wiring
- Authority mapping: map signer IDs→roles before calling `SigningPolicy.allows` (see [pms_govern/config/Rules_roles.yaml](pms_govern/config/Rules_roles.yaml)).
- Integration slice: run [pms_govern/integration_slice.py](pms_govern/integration_slice.py) for QGED→ledger→execution→evidence; relies on [pms_govern/immutable_ledger.py](pms_govern/immutable_ledger.py), [pms_govern/minimal_execution.py](pms_govern/minimal_execution.py), [pms_govern/execution_evidence.py](pms_govern/execution_evidence.py).

Process notes
- When adding events/actions/validators, update [CARSHUNTER/PMS_rules_v2.json](CARSHUNTER/PMS_rules_v2.json) first; code should stay data-driven off that file and the naming spec.
- Word templates at repo root (invoice/offer/logistics) must embed canonical doc_id and follow naming rules.
- Validators phrasing: required validators must be applied on all writes (`IdPattern`, `PathMatchesSpec`, `CreditNotExceedInvoice`).

Execution Checklists (Mutable, Append-Only)
- These checklists are operational aids. They do not define law, authority, or guarantees. If a checklist conflicts with the canonical reference, the checklist is wrong.
- Allowed: operational confirmations, verification steps, human reminders, "did you check X?"; forbidden: new authority rules, new STOP conditions, reinterpretation of doctrine, claims of completeness. For new STOP rules, change canon or rules JSON, not this checklist section.

### Checklist — Document Issuance (v2026-02-11)
Status: ACTIVE  
Derived from: CARSHUNTER_1447_CANONICAL_REFERENCE_v1.0  
Scope: Post-MRN document issuance only  
Change reason: Initial issuance checklist captured for auditability

- [ ] MRN = ISSUED (verified via registry)
- [ ] Invoice + VIN match single-truth anchors
- [ ] Disclosure profile selected (PUBLIC / REGULATOR / INTERNAL)
- [ ] Artifact rendered once (no previews)
- [ ] Artifact hashed (SHA256)
- [ ] Manifest created and linked
- [ ] Ledger append succeeded
- [ ] Distribution only via registry gate

### Checklist — Ledger & CLI Diagnostics (v2026-02-11)
Status: ACTIVE  
Derived from: CARSHUNTER_1447_CANONICAL_REFERENCE_v1.0  
Scope: Diagnosing ledger/CLI issues without mutating canonical artifacts  
Change reason: Provide safe diagnostic steps and prevent manual ledger edits or QGED bypass

Safety rules (non-negotiable):
- [ ] Never edit `ledger.jsonl` manually
- [ ] Never bypass QGED for "testing"
- [ ] Never generate distributable "preview" artifacts outside seal+ledger alignment
- [ ] If unsure, create a new timestamped work dir and reproduce there

A) Ledger integrity check
- [ ] Confirm you are not in a frozen/canonical directory (work only in timestamped work dir)
- [ ] Run ledger integrity verification (use `verify_integrity()` or provided verifier entrypoint)
- [ ] If integrity fails: STOP (diagnostic halt) — do not "repair" by editing; isolate and escalate to recovery procedure
- [ ] Capture failure evidence (stack trace + ledger head/tail hashes) into a timestamped diagnostic note

B) Append failure triage
- [ ] Confirm filesystem permissions and available disk space
- [ ] Confirm single-writer behavior (no concurrent process writing)
- [ ] Confirm last entry hash matches expected chain head
- [ ] Retry append once only in a fresh process; if it fails again: STOP and isolate

C) CLI sanity check (stub-aware)
- [ ] Confirm CLI is wired to the real engine (not stub defaults)
- [ ] Confirm required args/config are provided (no "empty GovernEngine()" instantiation)
- [ ] If CLI references missing functions (e.g., `evaluate_exposure`): mark as STUB and do not use for claims
- [ ] Use integration slice (QGED→ledger→execution→evidence) as the reference path when CLI is incomplete

D) Outcome logging (diagnostic evidence)
- [ ] Write a `DIAG_LEDGER_CLI_<timestamp>.md` note with: what you ran, what failed, hashes/entry ids, and next action
- [ ] Do not include real client identifiers / VIN / MRN / ACID unless AA explicitly approves

Maintenance checklist (mutable; append-only)
- Confirm change is non-canonical; if it alters invariants, stop and edit [CARSHUNTER_1447_CANONICAL_REFERENCE_v1.0.md](CARSHUNTER_1447_CANONICAL_REFERENCE_v1.0.md) only via approved path.
- Anchor every spec edit to a source in rules/naming/regulator docs; cite the file you relied on.
- Append new gaps/TODOs here instead of overwriting prior items; date-stamp if ambiguity risk.
- Reflect code changes: link to touched files/paths and note required validators or schema updates.
- After execution changes, re-run [pms_govern/integration_slice.py](pms_govern/integration_slice.py) and record outcomes in this spec.

Forensics/search guidance
- For internal forensics only (non-export, non-distribution): if searching vehicles (CARS/AUTO), ask for scope (time, locations, vehicle types) then search user folders recursively for pdf/docx/doc/xlsx/xls/zip/md/txt/json; match VIN and brand keywords.

CLI and exposure TODOs
- Implement or stub `evaluate_exposure` before using CLI diagnose/allow flow.
- Provide proper constructor inputs for `GovernEngine` within CLI once authority/ledger plumbing is wired.

### Standard Layout Rule — Offers (v2026-02-12)
Status: ACTIVE  
Scope: Offer renderings (HTML)  
Change reason: Align offers to a consistent dark blue + gold hero/stats/grid layout; avoid remote image dependencies

- Palette: dark blue gradient (#0f2027/#203a43/#2c5364) + gold (#d4af37) accents.
- Structure: hero with price + period CTA, stats strip, grid cards for feature groups, registry note, signature.
- Media: embed local render via relative path; do not rely on remote `mb.vin` fetches.
- Example/template: [CARSHUNTER_OFFER_S580_2026.html](CARSHUNTER_OFFER_S580_2026.html) embodies this standard.
- Registry note: keep `/events` `OFFER_CREATED` guidance in the layout; registry assigns doc_id/path.
